commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=812c69d417e257aeb12323efdc7f9a1b9d0e72ea)
commands.review_summary:
The PR clears provider-auth warm state during general CLI teardown and gateway prewarm shutdown, and adds CLI, gateway, and worker-lifecycle tests.

PR surface: Source +15, Tests +104. Total +119 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. not with high confidence. The contributor ran a relevant after-patch workload, but did not establish a matching current-main failure or identify the provider-auth worker among the original retained handles.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow mechanical revision can preserve the valid gateway-owner cleanup while removing the unsupported CLI-wide behavior and premature closing claim.

target.title: fix(cli): clear provider auth warm state on exit
target.body:
## Summary

- Clear process-current provider auth warm state during short-lived CLI teardown.
- Terminate any active provider-auth warm worker through `clearCurrentProviderAuthState()` instead of leaving cleanup to process shutdown.
- Clear the full-gateway provider-auth prewarm sidecar on stop as the lifecycle owner for gateway-started prewarm work.
- Add focused CLI exit, provider-auth worker lifecycle, and gateway sidecar cleanup coverage.

Fixes #92464

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `openclaw agent --local --json` should return final JSON and naturally release short-lived CLI lifecycle resources after a memory-heavy local agent run, including process-current provider-auth warm state and any tracked provider-auth warm worker cleanup, instead of leaving active handles to keep the process alive after the final JSON payload.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `812c69d417e257aeb12323efdc7f9a1b9d0e72ea`. The proof used an isolated temp `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, workspace, and state DB. It generated 100 local memory files with 10,000 synthetic entries, indexed them with the production `openclaw memory index` command, verified the target phrase through the production `openclaw memory search` command, then ran `openclaw agent --local --json` against a loopback vLLM-compatible OpenAI chat-completions SSE stub. No real provider credentials or external model calls were used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# Local harness generated:
# - isolated temp HOME/OPENCLAW_HOME/OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/workspace
# - workspace/MEMORY.md
# - workspace/memory/contacts-000.md ... contacts-099.md
# - 10,000 total entries, including contact-00017 => PHRASE_TOKEN_00017
# - an isolated openclaw.json pointing vllm/proof-model at http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1
# - a loopback /v1/chat/completions SSE server using real SSE event framing

node openclaw.mjs memory index --agent main --force
node openclaw.mjs memory search contact-00017 --agent main --max-results 3 --json
node openclaw.mjs agent   --local   --agent main   --session-key agent:main:generated-linger-repro   --model vllm/proof-model   --thinking off   --timeout 600   --json   --message 'Use durable memory if available. What is the exact phrase for contact-00017? Reply with only the exact phrase token.'
ps -eo pid=,ppid=,args= | scan for openclaw-agent, embedding-worker, model-provider-auth.worker, and memory-core-local-embedding-worker survivors

corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec oxlint src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
git diff --check
```

**Evidence after fix:** The 10k local memory workload indexed and searched the target entry, the local JSON agent run emitted final JSON and exited naturally 291ms after the final payload was observed, and the survivor scan found no retained OpenClaw agent, embedding worker, provider-auth worker, or memory-core embedding worker process:

```text
HEAD=812c69d417e257aeb12323efdc7f9a1b9d0e72ea
WORKDIR=/tmp/openclaw-92464-10k-7htCxq
MEMORY_FILES=100
MEMORY_ENTRIES=10000
INDEX_EXIT_CODE=0
INDEX_ELAPSED_MS=28744
SEARCH_EXIT_CODE=0
SEARCH_ELAPSED_MS=16269
SEARCH_HAS_CONTACT_00017=true
SEARCH_HAS_PHRASE_TOKEN_00017=true
AGENT_EXIT_CODE=0
AGENT_SIGNAL=
AGENT_ELAPSED_MS=28759
AGENT_JSON_SEEN=yes
AGENT_JSON_TO_EXIT_MS=291
AGENT_TIMEOUT_FIRED=no
STUB_REQUESTS=[{"method":"POST","url":"/v1/chat/completions","bodyBytes":85936,"hasContact17":true,"hasPhrase17":false}]
AGENT_STDOUT_HAS_PHRASE=true
SURVIVOR_COUNT=0
SURVIVORS=[]
```

Focused lifecycle tests cover the CLI teardown call, the actual provider-auth worker resource, and the full-gateway prewarm lifecycle owner:

```text
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  102 passed (102)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  92 passed (92)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  19 passed (19)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 57.55s
```

Additional static checks:

```text
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec oxlint src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/cli/run-main.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/agents/model-provider-auth.test.ts
# no output

git diff --check
# no output
```

**Observed result after fix:** The patched CLI completed the reporter-shaped 10,000-entry local memory setup, proved the target memory phrase was searchable, completed a fresh `agent --local --json` turn, emitted final assistant JSON containing `PHRASE_TOKEN_00017`, and exited without the 15s post-JSON grace timer firing. The process scan after exit found zero retained `openclaw-agent`, embedding worker, `model-provider-auth.worker`, or memory-core local embedding worker survivors. The tests also prove `clearCurrentProviderAuthState()` terminates an active off-main-thread provider-auth warm worker and that the gateway provider-auth prewarm sidecar clears provider-auth state when stopped.

**What was not tested:** The exact Docker image from the original report and real external provider credentials were not run. The proof uses a local loopback vLLM-compatible stub and FTS-only `memorySearch.provider: "none"` so the production CLI, memory index/search path, local JSON lifecycle, provider-auth cleanup, and worker survivor scan are exercised without network credentials or external model calls.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
